all.en: My job as a software engineer keeps me staring at a computer screen for ten hours a day.
all.zh: 我擔任軟體工程師的工作，讓我每天得盯著電腦螢幕長達十個小時。

all.en: My therapist suggested that I try a creative activity to release work-related stress.
all.zh: 我的治療師建議我嘗試一項創造性的活動，來釋放工作上的壓力。

all.en: I signed up for a beginner pottery class at a small studio near my office.
all.zh: 我在辦公室附近的一間小工作室報名了陶藝初學者課程。

all.en: The studio smelled of wet clay and felt peacefully quiet compared to my noisy workplace.
all.zh: 工作室散發著濕泥土的氣味，比起我嘈雜的職場顯得格外寧靜。

all.en: Our instructor was a gentle woman in her sixties with strong, skillful hands.
all.zh: 我們的指導老師是一位六十多歲、有著一雙強壯靈巧雙手的溫和女士。

all.en: She showed us how to center the clay on the spinning wheel before shaping it.
all.zh: 她示範了如何在塑形之前將陶土在轉盤上定心。

all.en: My first attempt collapsed into a wet, shapeless lump within thirty seconds.
all.zh: 我的第一次嘗試在三十秒內就塌成了一團濕漉漉、毫無形狀的泥巴。

all.en: The instructor laughed kindly and explained that even masters started with countless failures.
all.zh: 指導老師親切地笑了笑，並解釋說即使是大師也是從無數次失敗開始的。

all.en: After several lessons, I successfully made a small bowl that actually looked like a bowl.
all.zh: 經過幾堂課之後，我成功做出了一個看起來真的像碗的小碗。

all.en: Working with my hands forces me to slow down and focus only on the present moment.
all.zh: 用雙手操作迫使我放慢腳步，只專注於當下的這一刻。

all.en: I leave each class feeling calmer, lighter and reconnected with my creative side.
all.zh: 每次下課離開時，我都感覺更平靜、更輕盈，也重新連結了自己的創意一面。

all.en: Pottery has become a precious weekly ritual that protects my mental health.
all.zh: 陶藝已成為一項珍貴的每週儀式，守護著我的心理健康。
